title: Loan A Lisa

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Opening scene establishing Homer's character
jokes.punchline: Homer's iconic catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer catchphrase - high character integration and economy, moderate overall craft

jokes.text: No fair! We just went to church! Yeah, so we've already heard stories from thousands of years ago about stuff that didn't happen.
jokes.setup: Kids complaining about visiting Grampa
jokes.punchline: Equating church stories with Grampa's old tales as fictional
jokes.explanation: Sharp religious satire with good character voice - Lisa's intellectual skepticism coming through

jokes.text: I've had a lot of time to think since my hands got too shaky to play cards.
jokes.setup: Grampa announcing important news
jokes.punchline: Reason for deep thinking is inability to play cards
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - typical Grampa blend of pathos and absurdity

jokes.text: It's not your fault, Grampa. These cards are too slippery. See? [cards fall] Damn it!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to console Grampa about shaky hands
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately proves the point by dropping the cards himself
jokes.explanation: Classic physical comedy with good ironic structure

jokes.text: 50 bucks each?! That's your life savings?! This won't even cover the vig on what I owe!
jokes.setup: Family expecting a meaningful inheritance
jokes.punchline: Pathetically small amount, Homer's gambling debt reference
jokes.explanation: Good character moment revealing Homer's financial irresponsibility

jokes.text: Well, I lost the rest investing in a Broadway musical based on the real-life story of Eddie Gaedel, the midget who had one big league at-bat.
jokes.setup: Explaining where the money went
jokes.punchline: Incredibly specific and obscure baseball reference as basis for a Broadway musical
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist concept with real historical knowledge - very Simpsons-style specific reference

jokes.text: ♪ What do I do with this damn little man? His strike zone's the size of a tuna fish can! ♪
jokes.setup: Grampa singing from his failed Broadway musical
jokes.punchline: Hilariously specific lyric about baseball strike zone
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist Broadway song with perfect rhyme and rhythm - high craft execution

jokes.text: I just paid some loser 50 bucks to walk up the down escalator all day.
jokes.setup: Showing how the inheritance money is being spent
jokes.punchline: Completely wasteful and pointless use of money that's perfectly Bart
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart character moment - wasteful, mischievous, and oddly creative

jokes.text: I bet it's 11:00. I bet it's 11:00! Oh, 9:15?!
jokes.setup: Bart betting on something
jokes.punchline: Completely wrong guess, likely about the escalator walker's situation
jokes.explanation: Minor visual gag, functional but not exceptional

jokes.text: Ooh, so many celebrities have their own label: Princess Penelope, Booberella, The Real Housewives of Ogdenville, even Scratchy.
jokes.setup: Marge shopping for handbags
jokes.punchline: Mix of fake celebrities including Simpsons cartoon character Scratchy
jokes.explanation: Solid satire of celebrity brands with good meta element

jokes.text: Gotta look without looking like I'm looking. Yes!
jokes.setup: Marge trying to check the price secretly
jokes.punchline: Universal shopping behavior expressed perfectly
jokes.explanation: Relatable observational humor with good character voice

jokes.text: Oh, my God, you are so burned! I'll take it!
jokes.setup: Marge being embarrassed about not being able to afford the bag
jokes.punchline: Helen's gleeful cruelty followed by immediately buying the bag herself
jokes.explanation: Perfect Helen Lovejoy moment - petty, mean, and hypocritical

jokes.text: But stay on my good side, girlfriend. Or... uh-oh!
jokes.setup: Julio offering to be Marge's shopping companion
jokes.punchline: Stereotypical gay best friend behavior with threat
jokes.explanation: Typical character moment, somewhat stereotypical but in character

jokes.text: $500?! That's, like, ten dead Grampas!
jokes.setup: Homer shocked at handbag price
jokes.punchline: Bart's morbid math using Grampa's inheritance as unit of measurement
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor callback with perfect Bart logic - high craft and impact

jokes.text: Is that the cat in there?! Well, it's a cat. I'm not sure it's the cat.
jokes.setup: Homer asking about something in the carpet cleaner
jokes.punchline: Marge's distinction between 'a cat' and 'the cat' implying uncertainty about whose cat
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist wordplay with disturbing implications

jokes.text: It's not what you think! I'm researching a movie where I play myself.
jokes.setup: Celebrity at fancy restaurant
jokes.punchline: Absurd excuse for being at expensive place
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about celebrity behavior and method acting

jokes.text: After Bart, that bag's the best mistake we ever made.
jokes.setup: Homer commenting on the nice treatment they're getting
jokes.punchline: Calling Bart a mistake while comparing him to a handbag
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer insensitive comment with perfect economy and dark humor

jokes.text: I am just a goat, but even I know that a peasant in an emerging economy can't get a break. She needs a microloan.
jokes.setup: Educational video about microfinance
jokes.punchline: Goat delivering sophisticated economic analysis
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor contrasting animal with complex economics

jokes.text: And now, apparently, I am a girl.
jokes.setup: Goat narrating the educational video
jokes.punchline: Breaking fourth wall about animation inconsistency
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke about animation errors with good economy

jokes.text: I need backup, Lou. Get two plates and follow me. Uh, Chief, not now; I'm undercover.
jokes.setup: Chief Wiggum approaching the buffet
jokes.punchline: Lou claiming to be undercover at a buffet
jokes.explanation: Good character-based absurdist humor with police incompetence

jokes.text: Oh, it's like that, huh? The minute you go undercover, you act like you don't even know me! He'll turn on you, too, Fat Tony, you just wait!
jokes.setup: Wiggum upset about Lou ignoring him
jokes.punchline: Wiggum's paranoid escalation and addressing Fat Tony
jokes.explanation: Good Wiggum character moment with paranoid escalation

jokes.text: Boy, I bet that looked cool in super slow motion. Too bad we saw it in regular motion.
jokes.setup: Something dramatic happening at the restaurant
jokes.punchline: Wiggum commenting on action movie tropes vs. reality
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-commentary on action movie conventions

jokes.text: To find an entrepreneur you want to lend to, click on my nose... if you can catch it. It's up here! Now down here! Too slow!
jokes.setup: Nobel Prize winner explaining website interface
jokes.punchline: Distinguished economist acting like a cartoon character
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist contrast between serious economist and silly behavior

jokes.text: My name is Nelson Mandela Muntz.
jokes.setup: Nelson introducing himself for the microloan
jokes.punchline: Nelson's full name includes 'Mandela'
jokes.explanation: Good character detail with ironic name choice for a bully

jokes.text: My dream is to make enough money so's I can get the rest of this tattoo removed.
jokes.setup: Nelson explaining his business dreams
jokes.punchline: Dream is to undo a past mistake rather than achieve something
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Nelson's rough background with modest goals

jokes.text: Nelson, I gotta take this computer to the pawn shop, so's we can get White Castle for your birthday.
jokes.setup: Nelson trying to present professionally
jokes.punchline: His mother's poverty interrupting with pathetic birthday plans
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor showing family's economic situation

jokes.text: Some anonymous dude sent me 50 bucks. Probably someone cool like Famous Amos or Ba Ba Booey.
jokes.setup: Nelson discussing his mysterious benefactor
jokes.punchline: Nelson's idea of 'cool' people - cookie maker and radio producer
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing Nelson's limited cultural awareness

jokes.text: Good day, Mr. Barf-Breath. Good day to you. Let me be frank: Everyone thinks you're a wuss.
jokes.setup: Nelson trying to be professional with a customer
jokes.punchline: Immediately insulting the customer while trying to be polite
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nelson character moment - trying to be professional but still a bully

jokes.text: Tonight I'm having peanut butter and jelly. No more PB or J for me.
jokes.setup: Nelson celebrating his first business success
jokes.punchline: Modest celebration of affording a complete sandwich
jokes.explanation: Touching and funny moment showing Nelson's poverty and small dreams

jokes.text: I believe this stranger!
jokes.setup: Marge returning the handbag
jokes.punchline: Employee's completely trusting response to obvious lie
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor about retail naivety

jokes.text: You got to enjoy that bag for nothing! We can buy stuff we can't afford, temporarily own the hell out of it, and then indignantly bring it back for a full refund.
jokes.setup: Homer realizing the return policy loophole
jokes.punchline: Homer's scheme to abuse return policies
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer scheme with relatable consumer behavior satire

jokes.text: Hide a sandwich in this hat. 'Mary Poppins' myself to work with this umbrella.
jokes.setup: Homer planning his return scheme
jokes.punchline: Absurd uses for fancy items before returning them
jokes.explanation: Creative absurdist humor with good pop culture reference

jokes.text: Yeah, the luck lasts longer! Thanks to the power of returns, this store is our closet!
jokes.setup: Marge warning about pushing luck
jokes.punchline: Homer's twisted logic about luck and treating store as personal closet
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic with escalating absurdity

jokes.text: I'm making money fist over face! Dingus! Great timing! Awesome! No way I can look nerdy on this bike.
jokes.setup: Nelson's business update
jokes.punchline: Nelson saying 'fist over face' instead of 'hand over fist'
jokes.explanation: Good character-appropriate malapropism showing Nelson's limited vocabulary

jokes.text: This place is so fancy, Nelson. The ketchup packets are made of glass.
jokes.setup: Nelson taking his mother to a fancy restaurant
jokes.punchline: Mom's perspective on what constitutes fancy
jokes.explanation: Good character humor showing the family's unfamiliarity with upscale dining

jokes.text: Don't wipe your feet. Whoa, look at all this stuff! Did you find a mouse head in your Cuppa-Soup? I wish.
jokes.setup: Homer and Marge visiting Nelson's place
jokes.punchline: Homer's bizarre questions and Marge wishing for the settlement
jokes.explanation: Absurdist humor with typical Homer randomness

jokes.text: With this protective plastic overlay, I can't tell if I'm watching an old Seinfeld or an old Old Christine.
jokes.setup: Homer commenting on Nelson's TV setup
jokes.punchline: Specific TV show reference about visual similarity through plastic
jokes.explanation: Decent observational humor with specific pop culture references

jokes.text: I owe you everything. Well, I didn't do this for thanks, but if you want to thank me, I can stand here and listen for as long as it takes.
jokes.setup: Nelson wanting to thank Lisa
jokes.punchline: Lisa's false modesty immediately contradicted by her desire for praise
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lisa's ego despite her altruism

jokes.text: I never would have achieved the kind of success that has allowed me to quit school. You're quitting school?! Dropping it like a melon off an overpass.
jokes.setup: Nelson thanking Lisa for his success
jokes.punchline: Success has led to the wrong conclusion about education
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony and character-appropriate metaphor from Nelson

jokes.text: Spelling, meet F-Y-R-E.
jokes.setup: Nelson demonstrating his success
jokes.punchline: Nelson's creative but incorrect spelling of 'fire'
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay showing Nelson's limited education while trying to be clever

jokes.text: With Nelson gone, lunch money's finally making its way to the cafeteria. Nurples are returning to their natural, non-purple hue.
jokes.setup: Skinner explaining benefits of Nelson being gone
jokes.punchline: Wordplay on 'purple nurples' and their literal color
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with good observational humor about school bullying

jokes.text: $11 an hour? That's night watchman money.
jokes.setup: Learning about Nelson's wages
jokes.punchline: Skinner's specific reference point for good wages
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing Skinner's modest expectations

jokes.text: Isn't that a Christmas tie? It's a conversation starter!
jokes.setup: Skinner choosing a tie to impress Nelson
jokes.punchline: Skinner's misguided fashion choice
jokes.explanation: Typical Skinner awkwardness with modest humor

jokes.text: Sir, this suitcase has an airline tag on it, these ping-pong balls have been served and volleyed, and this hot plate's still warm.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to return obviously used items
jokes.punchline: Increasingly obvious evidence of use
jokes.explanation: Good escalating evidence of Homer's abuse of return policy

jokes.text: I'm Chris Hansen from To Catch a Credit-Whore.
jokes.setup: Store supervisor revealing himself
jokes.punchline: Parody of 'To Catch a Predator' for return fraud
jokes.explanation: Excellent parody concept with perfect execution and economy

jokes.text: I have to sign a release so you can broadcast this, right? Initial there, and... Never!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to escape the sting operation
jokes.punchline: Homer understanding TV law but still being tricked
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor about reality TV with typical Homer logic

jokes.text: S'up, Zuck? 'Mark Zuckerberg is... happy to meet new friends.'
jokes.setup: Meeting Mark Zuckerberg
jokes.punchline: Zuckerberg speaking like a Facebook status update
jokes.explanation: Clever satire of Facebook culture and social media speak

jokes.text: Well, the truth is... I dropped out of Harvard. You did? Better earnin' than learnin'!
jokes.setup: Lisa hoping for pro-education message
jokes.punchline: Zuckerberg confirming dropping out, Nelson's crude rhyme
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony with character-appropriate response from Nelson

jokes.text: I did. Graduated magna cum lade. I'm afraid I haven't done too well for myself. But I can certainly appreciate the irony of it.
jokes.setup: Someone who stayed in school
jokes.punchline: Highly educated but unsuccessful person appreciating the irony
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about education vs. success with meta-awareness

jokes.text: I gave a bum a dollar once, and he used it to buy an Ellery Queen mystery. But there was one mystery Ellery Queen couldn't solve-- why a man was named Ellery.
jokes.setup: Homer sharing a story about helping someone
jokes.punchline: Bizarre tangent about detective character's name
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer tangential thinking with absurdist observation

jokes.text: I gave a bum a dollar once, and he used it to buy an Ellery Queen mystery. But there was one mystery Ellery Queen couldn't solve-- why a man was named Ellery.
jokes.setup: Homer making a point about money not changing people
jokes.punchline: Gets distracted by the absurdity of the name Ellery
jokes.explanation: Good Homer tangent - starts with wisdom then gets derailed by random observation

jokes.text: My Wizard of Oz bike fell apart in the middle of the Pride parade! What kind of pride? Never you mind what kind!
jokes.setup: Angry customer complaining about bike failure
jokes.punchline: Awkward exchange about which pride parade
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor with social awkwardness

jokes.text: Uh, this epoxy you've been using is water soluble, which means it's not weather-resistant. Dumb it down for me. Um... bike... sticky... water go bye-bye?
jokes.setup: Martin explaining technical problem
jokes.punchline: Martin's attempt to explain simply to Nelson
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast with Martin's intelligence vs Nelson's comprehension

jokes.text: You can't break us up. We're like a family here. Kearney and I are having brunch on Sunday. If I'm up.
jokes.setup: Bullies not wanting Nelson to leave
jokes.punchline: Bullies having brunch plans with casual condition
jokes.explanation: Excellent character humor - tough bullies having sophisticated social plans

jokes.text: $50! I can buy paint for the art class, instead of those crushed-up berries I find in the woods.
jokes.setup: Skinner getting money for school supplies
jokes.punchline: Revealing the pathetic state of school art supplies
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing school's poverty with absurdist detail

jokes.text: Haw-haw! Sorry. Haw-haw! Sorry.
jokes.setup: Romantic moment between Nelson and Lisa
jokes.punchline: Nelson's involuntary trademark laugh interrupting the moment
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Nelson can't control his nature even in tender moments

jokes.text: 'Mark Zuckerberg is...' [crash sound]
jokes.setup: End credits scene
jokes.punchline: Zuckerberg still speaking in status updates during physical comedy
jokes.explanation: Decent callback with visual comedy element

jokes.text: Haw-haw! Sorry.
jokes.setup: Final credits continuation
jokes.punchline: Nelson's laugh continues into credits
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective character running gag to close episode

tmdb_overview: Grampa gives each family member a portion of his savings; Lisa worries that Nelson's instant success with a new business venture might lure him away from continuing his education.
